consensus: False-success risk is real: launch counts and result-file counts can mark success/complete even when partial or failed child outcomes exist.
Partial/failure semantics are inconsistently represented across commander spec, launcher state, monitor logic, and SQL/docs.
Bundle/report shapes do not fully encode why partial/failure happened or the counts needed to audit it.
conflicts: Commander spec says zero-launch should be failed, but launcher preflight currently records blocked.
README leans partial for under-launch while SKILL allows partial or failed without a precise threshold.

findings: Commander success is gated only on launch counts (50 squad leads, 250 workers), so a bundle can still read success after child failures accumulate.
Launch-proof fields use started terminology while success validation uses launched counters, creating ambiguous proof semantics.
SKILL SQL state lacks a partial status for runs/tasks, so commander partial outcomes have no durable state representation.
Docs require partial or failed when the full child swarm cannot launch, but do not define the threshold or a commander-level recovery/escalation path.
README and SKILL disagree on when under-launch should be partial versus failed.
Launcher preflight writes blocked for commander state on auth/preflight failure, which conflicts with the commander rule that zero launched squad leads must be failed.
Commander spec itself correctly distinguishes partial for constrained launches and failed for zero-launch cases.
Bundle Shape example hardcodes status success and does not enumerate success|partial|failed.
Bundle example omits fields explaining why a bundle is partial/failed or what exact launch counts triggered it.
Docs require partial on banned-model fallback, but the bundle example has no explicit place to report that violation.
Monitor completion is based on raw result-file count, not result status, so partial/failed bundles still drive the UI to COMPLETE.
Final monitor rendering only colors success/done as green, but completion gating happens earlier and ignores bundle health.
